Some of us have been warning for a long time about "deepfaked" propaganda and other synthetic media. If governments don't legislate aggressively against how AI-generated content can be used, we'll be propelled into a world in which none of us can tell anymore what's real and what isn't.

TikTok videos have emerged showing hundreds of thousands marching for AfD in Germany

Fake news and AI-generated propaganda is multiplying online ahead of Germany’s federal elections.

https://www.euronews.com/my-europe/2025/02/07/fake-tiktok-videos-show-hundreds-of-thousands-marching-for-afd-in-germany

record.text:
"How much does the rain impact your brain-fog?"

Let me put it this way:

My brain went down the road of "It's weird...when [character] gets really closed in and vulnerable, he sounds kinda like me" mid-editing, and it took me a solid 5 seconds before logic kicked in and I went "...oh, I'm a FOOL!!"

I don't consider that a weird question at all. I think people vary in their intention and drive behind what pushes them to share things in certain ways. I have admiration for the people who happily share their photos in full public view on platforms like this, but I have the very same...

i would drop my username but i wanna separate my regular art with my brainrot twt acc dkljaldjsakljd and also.... i dont want my irl friends finding it.... (im sorry i love you but i have my pride jdksdjklssda)

images.alt: Public Health - Hospitals - Parking for Patients "Requiring hospitals in the State to offer parking at no cost for inpatients for the duration of the inpatient's admission and for outpatients attending a scheduled medical appointment for the duration of the appointment; and requiring hospitals in the State to display signage notifying inpatients and outpatients of the availability of parking at no cost."

record.text: HB 519 is the first bill from Del. Matthew Schindler & would require Md. hospitals to provide free parking to inpatients for the duration of their stay & to outpatients attending scheduled appointments for the duration of those appointments, + display signage.: mgaleg.maryland.gov/mgawebsite/L...

images.alt: Photo of Anna Kornbluh, professor of English at University of Illinois Chicago, but best-known right now for her book Immediacy: Or, The Style of Too Late Capitalism, which, among other things, spots the rise autofiction, Fleabag and front-facing cameras on phones as signal flares of our desperate moment. She's on Culture File with Colm Toibin to champion David Lynch's Mulholland Drive as something that will bring the award-winning novelist out of his comfort zone. Podcast tomorrow here: http://www.rte.ie/lyricfm/culture-file-weekly/

record.text: Coming up on Culture File, we're in The Comfort Zone watching Mulholland Drive. @annakornbluh.bsky.social ("Immediacy") joins Luke Clancy to wave Colm Tóibín off on a journey into David Lynch's vision of Hollywood's dream machine/mincer. RTÉ lyric fm Sat 6.30pm (& pods from the link in ALT text)

I’m so sorry to hear this. As a nurse I deal with this all the time. When an insurance company rejects a med stating it’s “not medically necessary” your doctor (or nurse) can file a letter of exception. If that fails they can appeal it. It’s a process but one I do for several patients on the daily.

record.bridgyOriginalText:
@lavaeolus @SafeguardingResearch
Woohoo, congrats! Here's my translation (split into small posts due to instance limit, sorry)

1/ News anchor: Climate scientists across the globe are worried what will happen with their data. Now president Trump is back in power, many of them wonder whether their data are still safe. Some repositories are already hard to access, and the scientists worry that they will eventually become completely inaccessible. Some researchers are already taking action.

images.alt: a 3D model of a critter in a grey space with grid on the ground. The critter has a round head with a snout, two ear-like horns, and some little cheek fluff/antennae below the horns. Its body is comprised of two poofy cottonball-esque spheres and a third is at the end of a long and very skinny tail. it has four dainty little legs with tiny little feet. This is a 3/4 angle.

images.alt: Alt text: a painting with black and green watercolor for the background. In the upper right hand corner is a round yellow moon with light reflecting around it. There are white dots in the sky, stars. There is a chicory plant as the main focus of the artwork. There is one large blue and purple flower on a long green stem, and three smaller flowers scattered around it. There are two leaves coming from the flower stem.

images.alt:
This is a four panel comic featuring Raye and Lothar.

The first panel has are coming in asking Raye "Hey Raye have you seen my hoodie?"

The second panel features Lothar standing up, looking unamused staring in silence at Raye.

The third panel cuts to Lothar standing near the bed where Raye is sitting while wearing his hoodie. 

The last panel shows Raye standing up holding Lothar's hand saying "our hoodie"

This is the second installment of the comic where Lothar and Mark meet up

First panel:
Lothar: Ready to go Mark?
Mark: yeah... Aren't you cold?
Lothar: a bit but Raye stole my hoodie.

Second panel 
Mark: ahh yes that is the law of the relationships. Your hoodies are hers. You should totally steal hers.
Lothar: haha I would but they're too small. 
Mark: AHH boo that would have been a sight.
 
Third panel 

Lothar: ah yes a real pity. But don't worry I got her back. 
Mark: how so? 

Third panel 

Raye is standing in front of her kitchen counter with her step stool out of reach. Her fist is shaking in the air.
Raye: Lothar you son of a B****h

external.description: "I want them to give us something for all of the money that we put up," Trump said on Feb. 22. "We're asking for rare earth and oil — anything we can get."
external.title: 'We're going to get our money back' — Trump on US-Ukraine resources deal

value.text:
⚡️'We're going to get our money back' — Trump on US-Ukraine resources deal. 

"I want them to give us something for all of the money that we put up," Trump said on Feb. 22. "We're asking for rare earth and oil — anything we can get."

record.text: My mom told me that in elementary school they told her that she might want to get me tested for autism but that they didn’t offer that service, plus I had such good grades (their words) that I didn’t qualify. So “low” support, but truly I needed much more.

Had a dream where all my stuff was stolen. Computer, playstation, wii u, 3DS, Switch, psp?? My pokewalker???? Even all my accounts were hacked, and on all my consoles, my saved data and information were erased. Slowly got everything back one-by-one.

Dreams are fucked.
